title: pseudoxandra sclerocarpa
intro: Pseudoxandra sclerocarpa is a plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family, native to the tropical regions of West Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Pseudoxandra sclerocarpa include its potential to treat fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antidote for snake bites. Herbal preparations of Pseudoxandra sclerocarpa are typically made by decocting the plant's roots and leaves in water, resulting in a medicinal tea or infusion.
